dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7580 IH: Nutrition First Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mrs. Luna introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Agriculture

form.official-title: To amend the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008 to prohibit the purchase of nutritionally deficient foods with supplemental nutrition assistance program benefits.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Nutrition First Act of 2026.
Section 3 of the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008 (7 U.S.C. 2012) is amended—

paragraph.text: in subsection (k) by striking food or food product each place it appears and inserting eligible food, and
by inserting after subsection (k) the following:

subsection.enum: (k–1) Eligible food means a food or beverage that meets nutritional standards established by the Secretary of Agriculture, excluding the following:

paragraph.text: A sugar-sweetened beverage commonly known as soda, or a nonalcoholic beverage containing sugar, cane sugar, corn syrup, high-fructose corn syrup, honey, or other caloric sweetener, excluding—

subparagraph.text: a beverage containing milk or a milk substitute;
a beverage with more than 50 percent fruit or vegetable juice; and
a product requiring preparation before consumption.

paragraph.text: A candy preparation consisting of sugar, honey, or other sweeteners combined with chocolate, fruit, nuts, or other ingredients in bar, drop, or piece form, excluding items containing flour or requiring refrigeration.

A high-sugar beverage that lists carbonated water plus any caloric sweetener (e.g. sugar, cane sugar, corn syrup, HFCS) as the first 2 ingredients listed, excluding aspartame or other non-caloric sweetener as the first 2 ingredients listed.
A prepared dessert or snack food, such as a cake, cookie, pastry, pie, doughnut, muffin, candy-coated snack, or similar product with a high level of added sugar, sodium, or saturated fat.

An energy drink or other beverage containing an added sweetener, and marketed or labeled as an energy drink, including a product containing a stimulant such as caffeine, taurine, guarana, or a similar additive.

section.text: This Act and the amendment made by this Act shall take effect 180 days after the date of the enactment of this Act.
